items.speaker: Dennis
items.text: I have lost. The elegance I stood by... was defeated?

items.speaker: Dennis
items.text: No... this is normal. After all, your dueling style possessed its own form of elegance. In a sense, elegance still triumphed...!

options.text: Is being elegant really that important?

items.speaker: Dennis
items.text: Is the grass green? Of course it is! Elegance is etiquette. Elegance is discipline. Elegance is order.

items.speaker: Dennis
items.text: Students must be disciplined and elegant while on campus. And we professors should be shining beacons of that ideal.

items.speaker: Dennis
items.text: Even club activities ought to require a professor's guiding hand, lest they descend into utter anarchy!

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Have you thought about being the Peaks of Prestige fan club's advisor, Professor?

items.speaker: Dennis
items.text: The thought has graced my mind. But an advisor must be sufficiently affable to maintain good relations with students in the fan club.

items.speaker: Dennis
items.text: But I am a disciplinarian! I must maintain an aura of unyielding authority! So I rejected such an idea.

items.speaker: Dennis
items.text: Besides, I'm... not exactly a people person. I can deduct points according to Academy guidelines without problem, but beyond that, I struggle to interact with the students.

items.speaker: Dennis
items.text: I'm aware the students regard me more with "abject terror" than anything close to fondness or respect.

options.text: You will never know until you try.
Dueling is also a form of communication.

items.speaker: Dennis
items.text: ...Indeed. Mr. Furius mentioned in his lecture that dueling can sometimes be far more effective than words.

items.speaker: Dennis
items.text: Fellow duelists tend to resonate with one another... Perhaps you're right. Instead of being a professor whom students merely fear, I could be one... whom they respect.

items.speaker: Dennis
items.text: But if I am to lead everyone, I cannot afford another defeat such as today.

items.speaker: Dennis
items.text: To become an advisor with skills so elegant that future club members weep at its beauty, I must continue to refine my dueling skills.

items.speaker: Dennis
items.text: As per the rules, you still need to defeat Professor Festa before your final match with Professor Calith.

items.speaker: Dennis
items.text: She has proven to be a terrifying duelist. Though her style may have all the elegance of a spreadsheet, her precision is as deadly as a machine.

items.speaker: Dennis
items.text: Be on your guard, {PlayerName}.

options.text: I'll keep that in mind. Thank you, Professor Dennis.

items.speaker: Festa
items.text: I... lost?! But all the input parameters were perfect... How could the results deviate this much...?

items.speaker: Festa
items.text: ...Unless... {PlayerName}, you're the one... Could this be what Mornye was talking about?

options.text: Professor Mornye said what?
Uh, I don't quite follow.

items.speaker: Festa
items.text: ...A tiny deviation, given enough calculations, can completely overwrite the expected future state.

items.speaker: Festa
items.text: Just like that variable I couldn't account for in my proposed model.

items.speaker: Festa
items.text: Even in a system as simple as Peaks of Prestige, it holds true. Though I can't figure out how you introduced a deviation that even I couldn't detect...

options.text: I refused to believe I could lose.
Calculations can't determine the future.

items.speaker: Festa
items.text: That's it? No... If I follow that logic... Are you implying that human will and emotion can interfere with theory to create unpredictable outcomes?

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: I've seen something like this before. Whether it's the Tethys System's simulations or so-called fated "prophecies," human will can defy predicted outcomes to create new futures.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Maybe that's what the "unknown variable" in your model really represents.

items.speaker: Festa
items.text: ...Very well, this is somewhat inspiring. But fundamentally, even if this theory is sound, this so-called "human factor" cannot even be quantified...

items.speaker: Festa
items.text: The model would need to account for infinite branching paths, like a snowflake's unique formation... Ah, never mind. I'll chew on it later.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: By the way, Professor Festa, you don't really seem to care too much about disbanding the Peaks of Prestige fan club, do you?

items.speaker: Festa
items.text: Of course not. I'm an ultra-hardcore duelist! I think Peaks of Prestige is the most interesting game to come out in years!

items.speaker: Festa
items.text: Oh, to watch my opponents' carefully designed strategies and decks walk right into my calculations, like bugs caught in a spider's web, helplessly awaiting their doom...

items.speaker: Festa
items.text: It's a victory for mathematics! It's exhilarating!

items.speaker: Festa
items.text: I have zero interest in disbanding any club. But Professor Calith promised that if I worked with him, I'd have no shortage of powerful duelists wanting to challenge me...

items.speaker: Festa
items.text: To be honest, he wasn't wrong. But out of all the challengers, only our duel was brilliantly satisfying.

items.speaker: Festa
items.text: This feeling of giving it your all, only to come up just short... it hurts, but it's not the worst, huh?

options.text: You win some, you lose some.
Where's the fun in a duel without any surprises?

items.speaker: Festa
items.text: Perhaps. Well then, my mathematical models need some modifications. Until next time, {PlayerName}.

items.speaker: Chessa
items.text: Phew... Finally. My job here is done.

items.speaker: Chessa
items.text: That's it for me. Bye-bye, everyone.

items.speaker: Olof
items.text: ...Wait!

items.speaker: Chessa
items.text: Huh? We don't have some complicated beef or anything... I was just backup Calith called in.

items.speaker: Chessa
items.text: Owed him a small favor, and now we're square. Come on, just let me go.

items.speaker: Olof
items.text: I have to know... how did you get so strong? What's your secret?

items.speaker: Chessa
items.text: Huh? Nothing special, really. I was on shift when I ran into Calith, and it looked like something was bugging him. I asked out of curiosity.

items.speaker: Chessa
items.text: Next thing I know, he's insisting I play Peaks of Prestige with him. After one game, he said I was a natural. Haha... do I look like a natural to you?

items.speaker: Chessa
items.text: He wanted me to play against this fan club. I figured it'd be a good way to pay him back too.

items.speaker: Chessa
items.text: Sigh... If I'd known it'd be this much of a hassle, I would've just looked the other way. But since I lost, guess I wasn't cut out for it. He can't complain.

items.speaker: Olof
items.text: ...H-Hassle? Don't you have any interest in Peaks of Prestige at all?

items.speaker: Chessa
items.text: Of course not. I hate anything that requires effort. And I'm always getting dragged into errands by my boss or some professor.

items.speaker: Chessa
items.text: If the Academy were under attack by Voidworms or a TD outbreak, I'd gladly put my life on the line to protect everyone.

items.speaker: Chessa
items.text: But this kind of meaningless scut work? No thanks.

options.text: ...Thanks for your, uh, hard work.

items.speaker: Chessa
items.text: Don't mention it... Well, I'd better get going. Bye-bye.
Chessa turns and walks away. You can almost see the weight of this meaningless task lifting from her shoulders with every step she takes.

items.speaker: Ashild
items.text: Olof...?

items.speaker: Olof
items.text: Oh, I'm fine. Well done, {PlayerName}. Thanks for helping our fan club.

items.speaker: Olof
items.text: Haha, who knew there was a hidden powerhouse like you at the Academy?

items.speaker: Olof
items.text: But that's not to say you can slack off. I may not be strong enough to beat you now, but one day, I'm taking back the title of No.2!

options.text: I look forward to it.
Don't expect me to go easy on you.

items.speaker: Olof
items.text: By the way, Anita said she'd go find Professor Calith to arrange the final showdown.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Understood. I'll head over right away.

items.speaker: Ashild
items.text: Good luck, {PlayerName}!

items.speaker: Calith
items.text: ...Well done, {PlayerName}. I admit defeat.

items.speaker: Calith
items.text: As agreed, I will withdraw the application to disband the fan club. But for your own good, and for the Academy's... I urge you to stop playing that cursed game.

options.text: What exactly happened in the past?

items.speaker: Anita
items.text: Right, Professor Calith, you said you knew someone who threw it all away for this game...?

items.speaker: Calith
items.text: ...Yes. The man who threw away his future, betrayed his studies, and neglected his responsibilities, all to indulge in pleasure...

items.speaker: Calith
items.text: Is none other than the man calling himself the founder of Peaks of Prestige, Furius!

options.text: What...?
You're kidding, right?

items.speaker: Calith
items.text: It's the truth. We went to school together... long before I ever came to this Academy.

items.speaker: Calith
items.text: Don't let his carefree act fool you. He was once a brilliant student. He was the one who should've ended up at the Academy, not me.

items.speaker: Calith
items.text: Though I hate to admit it, before he threw it all away, he was considered a genius by many...

items.speaker: Calith
items.text: Top marks without even trying. The teachers always praised him for it.

items.speaker: Calith
items.text: As for me... No matter how hard I tried, I was always regarded as someone standing in his shadow.

items.speaker: Calith
items.text: But it wasn't supposed to be like that! He was meant to be my rival! The one I would eventually surpass and defeat!

items.speaker: Calith
items.text: I spent all my days studying, pushing myself to beat him in exams and prove to everyone that I was the most outstanding student!

options.text: That's good, right?
A little rivalry is good, I guess.
That seems a bit extreme.

items.speaker: Calith
items.text: ...Just when I was on the verge of finally surpassing him, he just... dropped out of school!

items.speaker: Calith
items.text: He said he was going back to Septimont to "pursue his real passion." That bastard... it meant he'd remain undefeated forever!

items.speaker: Calith
items.text: If only he'd waited another month... No, another week! I could've shown everyone what defeat looked like on him!

options.text: I don't see how Furius was "indulging in pleasure."
I don't see who Furius betrayed.

items.speaker: Calith
items.text: You must be joking... Furius dropped out to create that Peaks of Prestige, did he not?!

items.speaker: Calith
items.text: I will never accept that! It's a betrayal of his academic potential... How infuriating that his silly game managed to become a success, too!

items.speaker: Calith
items.text: That guy... I thought becoming a professor here would finally make him see me as an equal, but he never took me seriously. Not once...

items.speaker: Anita
items.text: Ahem... I think I understand now. So you're that "dear friend and rival" Furius has mentioned before...

items.speaker: Calith
items.text: ...What?

items.speaker: Anita
items.text: Just a sec, let me find that interview Furius gave in Septimont...
Anita projects a video showing Furius.

items.speaker: Furius
items.text: How did I come up with Peaks of Prestige? Haha... I owe it all to a dear friend and rival of mine.

items.speaker: Furius
items.text: I'll keep his name a secret, but back in school, I had this very serious friend, a real perfectionist.

items.speaker: Furius
items.text: I actually got the inspiration for Peaks of Prestige during a tactical simulation practice with him.

items.speaker: Furius
items.text: He kept launching these fierce Echo attacks against my defenses. I was exhausted just trying to hold the line. But looking back now... that can be quite fun, agreed?

items.speaker: Furius
items.text: Haha... That was just one of many moments between us. He was always so driven. How could I afford to fall behind?

items.speaker: Furius
items.text: In fact, without the pressure he put on me, the constant criticism and praise, I probably would've started slacking off a long time ago. He always did call me out for being so nonchalant, hehe...
The projection ends. Calith's face is a mask of stunned disbelief.

items.speaker: Calith
items.text: Wait... Was that really Furius? No, that infuriating grin... that was definitely him.

items.speaker: Anita
items.text: I don't know what exactly happened between you two... But I don't think Furius ever looked down on you.

items.speaker: Anita
items.text: It's clear that your dedication and hard work put a lot of pressure on him. He must have been working hard in secret too... He just never showed it.

items.speaker: Calith
items.text: Tsk, I knew it... He may look like a slacker, but he was grinding behind my back all along. So he wasn't just some natural talent who didn't need to try...

items.speaker: Calith
items.text: Fine! Once I get the chance, I'm going straight to Septimont. I'll use his own Peaks of Prestige to beat him at his own game, and make him admit defeat to my face!

items.speaker: Anita
items.text: ...Impressive. Your skills speak for themselves, {PlayerName}.

items.speaker: Anita
items.text: Now, as division manager, I grant you the qualification to participate in the Lahai-Roi division Ranked Tournament.

options.text: So what exactly is this tournament?

items.speaker: Anita
items.text: Unlike the single-elimination "Apex Duelist Championship" held in Septimont, our divisions follow a points-based system.

items.speaker: Anita
items.text: Qualified duelists can challenge anyone on the tournament roster. Win, and you earn points.

items.speaker: Anita
items.text: When the season ends, the duelist with the highest score will be crowned the Division Champion.

options.text: I think I get the idea.
When does the tournament start?

items.speaker: Anita
items.text: I've already sent the coordinates of the other competitors to your Terminal. Feel free to challenge them anytime.

items.speaker: Anita
items.text: Good luck, {PlayerName}! I'm looking forward to the day you become our Division Champion.
